---
title: "zewwwwEcon: A Template for Econ Articles with Quarto"
pdf-engine: xelatex
author:
  - name: Lord Macbeth
    affiliation:
      - name: Thane of Glamis
  - name: Lady Macbeth
    acknowledgements: | 
      Corrseponding author: Adress, Place. 
    affiliation:
      - name: Queen of Scotland
  - name: First Witch
    affiliation:
      - name: Posters of the Sea
      - name: Posters of the Land
  - name: Second Witch
    affiliation:
      - name: Posters of the Sea
      - name: Posters of the Land
  - name: Third Witch
    affiliation:
      - name: Posters of the Sea
      - name: Posters of the Land
      
thanks: |
   We would like to thank ....  
   
prelim-note: "Preliminary draft - please do not distribute"
  
abstract: |
  O, never Shall sun that morrow see! Your face, my thane, is as a book where men May read strange matters. To beguile the time, Look like the time; bear welcome in your eye, Your hand, your tongue: look like the innocent flower, But be the serpent under't. He that's coming Must be provided for: and you shall put This night's great business into my dispatch; Which shall to all our nights and days to come Give solely sovereign sway and masterdom.
  
jel: "These are the JEL codes"
 
keywords: "These are the keywords"

date: today # Alternatively, enter a date as "1 May 2024"
date-format: "D MMMM YYYY"

number: "Version 0.1"
repohash: "#`r substr(system('git rev-parse HEAD', intern= TRUE),1,8)`" 
# To include hash from another repository use system('git ls-remote ../your_git_repository_folder | grep HEAD', intern=TRUE)[1]" 
  
format:
  zewwww-econ-pdf: default

color-links: "82,125,164" # RGB color for internal links and footnotes
# You can set an arbitrary RGB code here. Leave empty for black (default).
bibliography: ../references.bib
biblio-style: apa # Select from https://www.overleaf.com/learn/latex/Biblatex_bibliography_styles

add-tex: |
       \usepackage{lipsum}
   
editor: source
---

```{r Setup}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
# Reload Setup s.t. everything renders as intended
library(here)
source(here("B_Prog/0setup.R"))
```

# Introduction

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.[^1] If you are interested in incentives in health insurance risk adjustment schemes, check out @reifSettingIncentivesRight2025. You can easily refer to parts of the paper such as @fig-eventstudy or @tbl-regression2. Sometimes one might need equations. Just use \LaTeX\ for this in the text to show that $2^{2} > 3$. You can do it like this:

[^1]: Here, we have an excellent example of a footnote.

$$
\frac{\partial S(\beta)}{\partial \beta} = -2X^\top (y - X\beta) = 0
$$

$$
X^\top X \hat{\beta} = X^\top y
$$

$$
\hat{\bm{\beta}} = (X^\top X)^{-1} X^\top y
$$

Or just plot code: 
```{r program}
#| echo: true 
#| warning: false
#| message: false
# We can use code that is displayed in the output
2^2
2^2 > 3
```

{{< lipsum 2-4 >}}

# Figures


:::: {#fig-hist fig-pos="h" fig-cap="Distribution of height (in cm) in random data"}

```{r histogram, cache=TRUE, cache.extra=tools::md5sum(here("D_Out/g_hist.rds"))}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| fig-width: 3.5
#| fig-asp: 0.5
#| fig-align: center
g_hist <- readRDS(here("D_Out/g_hist.rds"))
g_hist
rm(g_hist)
```

::: {.notes-block}
**Notes:** You can use this text to provide further information about the table. Nunc sed pede. Praesent vitaelectus. Praesent neque justo, vehicula eget, interdum id, facilisis et, nibh. Phasellus at purus et libero laciniadictum. Fusce aliquet. Nulla eu ante placerat leo semper dictum. Mauris metus. Curabitur lobortis. Curabitursollicitudin hendrerit nunc. Donec ultrices lacus id ipsum.
:::
::::

{{< lipsum 2-4 >}}

:::: {#fig-barchart fig-pos="h" fig-cap="Number of Federal States by Country"}
```{r Barchart, cache=TRUE, cache.extra=tools::md5sum(here("D_Out/g_bar.rds"))}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| fig-width: 3.5
#| fig-asp: 0.5
#| fig-align: center
#| fig-pos: H
g_bar <- readRDS(here("D_Out/g_bar.rds"))
g_bar
rm(g_bar)
```

::: {.notes-block}
**Notes:** You can use this text to provide further information about the table. Nunc sed pede. Praesent vitaelectus. Praesent neque justo, vehicula eget, interdum id, facilisis et, nibh. Phasellus at purus et libero laciniadictum. Fusce aliquet. Nulla eu ante placerat leo semper dictum. Mauris metus. Curabitur lobortis. Curabitursollicitudin hendrerit nunc. Donec ultrices lacus id ipsum.
:::
::::

{{< lipsum 1-2 >}}

:::: {#fig-ts1 fig-pos="h" fig-cap="Displaying how things evolve over time"}
```{r tsplot1, cache=TRUE, cache.extra=tools::md5sum(here("D_Out/g_tsplot.rds"))}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| fig-width: 3
#| fig-asp: 0.5
#| fig-align: center
#| fig-pos: H
g_tsplot <- readRDS(here("D_Out/g_tsplot.rds"))
g_tsplot
rm(g_tsplot)
```

::: {.notes-block}
**Notes:** You can use this text to provide further information about the figure. Nunc sed pede. Praesent vitaelectus. Praesent neque justo, vehicula eget, interdum id, facilisis et, nibh. Phasellus at purus et libero laciniadictum. Fusce aliquet. Nulla eu ante placerat leo semper dictum. Mauris metus. Curabitur lobortis. Curabitursollicitudin hendrerit nunc. Donec ultrices lacus id ipsum.
:::
::::


{{< lipsum 1-2 >}}

:::: {#fig-scatterplot fig-pos="h" fig-cap="Two groups have very different values"}
```{r scatterplot, cache=TRUE, cache.extra=tools::md5sum(here("D_Out/g_scatterplot.rds"))}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| fig-width: 3.3
#| fig-asp: 0.5
#| fig-align: center
#| fig-pos: H
g_scatterplot <- readRDS(here("D_Out/g_scatterplot.rds"))
g_scatterplot
rm(g_scatterplot)
```

::: {.notes-block}
**Notes:** You can use this text to provide further information about the figure. Nunc sed pede. Praesent vitaelectus. Praesent neque justo, vehicula eget, interdum id, facilisis et, nibh. Phasellus at purus et libero laciniadictum. Fusce aliquet. Nulla eu ante placerat leo semper dictum. Mauris metus. Curabitur lobortis. Curabitursollicitudin hendrerit nunc. Donec ultrices lacus id ipsum.
:::
::::

{{< lipsum 1 >}}

:::: {#fig-dotplot fig-pos="h" fig-cap="Visualizing distributions with few observations"}
```{r dotplot, cache=TRUE, cache.extra=tools::md5sum(here("D_Out/g_dotplot.rds"))}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| fig-width: 3.5
#| fig-asp: 0.5
#| fig-align: center
#| fig-pos: H
g_dotplot <- readRDS(here("D_Out/g_dotplot.rds"))
g_dotplot
rm(g_dotplot)
```

::: {.notes-block}
**Notes:** You can use this text to provide further information about the figure. Nunc sed pede. Praesent vitaelectus. Praesent neque justo, vehicula eget, interdum id, facilisis et, nibh. Phasellus at purus et libero laciniadictum. Fusce aliquet. Nulla eu ante placerat leo semper dictum. Mauris metus. Curabitur lobortis. Curabitursollicitudin hendrerit nunc. Donec ultrices lacus id ipsum.
:::
::::

{{< lipsum 1-2 >}}

:::: {#fig-eventstudy fig-pos="h" fig-cap="Coefficients relative to treatment time"}
```{r eventstudy, cache=TRUE, cache.extra=tools::md5sum(here("D_Out/g_eventstudy.rds"))}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| fig-width: 3.2
#| fig-asp: 0.6
#| fig-align: center
#| fig-pos: H
g_eventstudy <- readRDS(here("D_Out/g_eventstudy.rds"))
g_eventstudy
rm(g_eventstudy)
```

::: {.notes-block}
**Notes:** You can use this text to provide further information about the figure. Nunc sed pede. Praesent vitaelectus. Praesent neque justo, vehicula eget, interdum id, facilisis et, nibh. Phasellus at purus et libero laciniadictum. Fusce aliquet. Nulla eu ante placerat leo semper dictum. Mauris metus. Curabitur lobortis. Curabitursollicitudin hendrerit nunc. Donec ultrices lacus id ipsum.
:::
::::


:::: {#fig-choro fig-pos="h" fig-cap="This is a choropleth map"}
```{r choro, cache=TRUE, cache.extra=tools::md5sum(here("D_Out/g_choro.rds"))}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| fig-width: 3.3
#| fig-asp: 0.5
#| fig-align: center
#| fig-pos: H
g_choro <- readRDS(here("D_Out/g_choro.rds"))
g_choro
rm(g_choro)
```

::: {.notes-block}
**Notes:** You can use this text to provide further information about the figure. Nunc sed pede. Praesent vitaelectus. Praesent neque justo, vehicula eget, interdum id, facilisis et, nibh. Phasellus at purus et libero laciniadictum. Fusce aliquet. Nulla eu ante placerat leo semper dictum. Mauris metus. Curabitur lobortis. Curabitursollicitudin hendrerit nunc. Donec ultrices lacus id ipsum.
:::
::::


:::: {#fig-indic fig-pos="h" fig-cap="This is an indicator map"}
```{r indic, cache=TRUE, cache.extra=tools::md5sum(here("D_Out/g_indic.rds"))}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| fig-width: 3.3
#| fig-asp: 0.5
#| fig-align: center
#| fig-pos: H
g_indic <- readRDS(here("D_Out/g_indic.rds"))
g_indic
rm(g_indic)
```

::: {.notes-block}
**Notes:** You can use this text to provide further information about the figure. Nunc sed pede. Praesent vitaelectus. Praesent neque justo, vehicula eget, interdum id, facilisis et, nibh. Phasellus at purus et libero laciniadictum. Fusce aliquet. Nulla eu ante placerat leo semper dictum. Mauris metus. Curabitur lobortis. Curabitursollicitudin hendrerit nunc. Donec ultrices lacus id ipsum.
:::
::::


# Tables

{{< lipsum 1-2 >}}

:::: {#tbl-descriptives tbl-pos="h" tbl-cap="Descriptive statistics by group"}
```{r descriptives}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| tbl-align: center
#| tbl-pos: T
t_descriptives <- readRDS(here("D_Out/t_descriptives.rds"))
t_descriptives
rm(t_descriptives)
```

::: {.notes-block}
**Notes:** Here is additional information on the table, which can be lengthy. It does not have to be but in order to check for line breaks, it makes sense to have it this way. It does not have to be but in order to check for line breaks, it makes sense to have it this way.
:::
::::

{{< lipsum 1-2 >}}

:::: {#tbl-regression tbl-pos="h" tbl-cap="Linear Regression Models"}
```{r regressions}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| tbl-align: center
#| tbl-pos: T
t_regression <- readRDS(here("D_Out/t_regression.rds"))
t_regression
rm(t_regression)
```

::: {.notes-block}
**Notes:** Here is additional information on the table, which can be lengthy. It does not have to be but in order to check for line breaks, it makes sense to have it this way. It does not have to be but in order to check for line breaks, it makes sense to have it this way.
:::
::::


{{< lipsum 1-2 >}}

# References {.unnumbered}

::: {#refs}
:::


{{< pagebreak >}}

# Appendix {.unnumbered}

::: {.appendix-setup letter="A"}
:::

:::: {#tbl-regression2 tbl-pos="h" tbl-cap="Linear Regression Models for the Appendix"}
```{r regressions2}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| tbl-align: center
#| tbl-pos: T
t_regression <- readRDS(here("D_Out/t_regression.rds"))
t_regression
rm(t_regression)
```

::: {.notes-block}
**Notes:** Here is additional information on the table, which can be lengthy. It does not have to be but in order to check for line breaks, it makes sense to have it this way. It does not have to be but in order to check for line breaks, it makes sense to have it this way.
:::
::::

:::: {#fig-two fig-pos="h" fig-cap="Complex figure with two parts"}
```{r jointplot, cache=TRUE, cache.extra=tools::md5sum(here("D_Out/g_tsplot.rds"))}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| fig-align: center
#| fig-subcap:
#|   - "Time Series"
#|   - "Scatterplot"
#| layout: [[-5, 35,-20, 35, -5]]
g_tsplot <- readRDS(here("D_Out/g_tsplot.rds"))
g_tsplot +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 26),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 26),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 26),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 26),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 26),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 26)
  )

g_scatterplot <- readRDS(here("D_Out/g_scatterplot.rds"))
g_scatterplot +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 26),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 26),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 26),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 26),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 26),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 26)
  )

rm(g_scatterplot)
rm(g_tsplot)
```

::: {.notes-block}
**Notes:** You can use this text to provide further information about the figure. Nunc sed pede. Praesent vitaelectus. Praesent neque justo, vehicula eget, interdum id, facilisis et, nibh. 
:::
:::::

:::: {#fig-complex fig-pos="h" fig-cap="Complex figure with three parts"}
```{r complexplot, cache=TRUE, cache.extra=tools::md5sum(here("D_Out/g_tsplot.rds"))}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| fig-align: center
#| fig-subcap:
#|   - "Time Series"
#|   - "Scatterplot"
#|   - "Histogram"
#|   - " "
#| layout: [[45,-10, 45], [100], [-35,30,-35]]
g_tsplot <- readRDS(here("D_Out/g_tsplot.rds"))
g_tsplot +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 16)
  )

g_scatterplot <- readRDS(here("D_Out/g_scatterplot.rds"))
g_scatterplot +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 16)
  )

g_hist <- readRDS(here("D_Out/g_hist.rds"))
g_hist + coord_fixed(ratio = 0.5) + theme(legend.position = "none")

as_ggplot(get_legend(g_hist + theme(legend.direction = "horizontal", legend.title = element_blank())))

rm(g_scatterplot)
rm(g_tsplot)
rm(g_hist)
```

::: {.notes-block}
**Notes:** You can use this text to provide further information about the figure. Nunc sed pede. Praesent vitaelectus. Praesent neque justo, vehicula eget, interdum id, facilisis et, nibh. 
:::
:::::

::::: {.landscape}
:::: {#tbl-regression3 tbl-pos="H" tbl-cap="Linear Regression Models as a Sideways Table"}
```{r regression3}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| tbl-align: center
#| tbl-pos: H
t_regression <- readRDS(here("D_Out/t_regression.rds"))
t_regression
rm(t_regression)
```
::: {.notes-block width = "1.6" above = "-5" below = "15"}
**Notes:** You can use this text to provide further information about the figure. Nunc sed pede. Praesent vitaelectus. Praesent neque justo, vehicula eget, interdum id, facilisis et, nibh. 
:::
::::
:::::
